title: lespedeza capitata var. calycina
intro: Lespedeza capitata var. calycina, also known as the purple bushclover, is a perennial legume plant native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its purple flowers and hairy stems. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Lespedeza capitata var. calycina include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine.
